title: Dependencies and reactive values

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific React: Component-Based Browser Applications draft for Dependencies and reactive values; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: React Learn and Reference; React 19.2; provisions applicable to Dependencies and reactive values
sources.claim: Effect dependencies list every reactive value read so synchronization is reapplied when those values change.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Effect dependencies list every reactive value read so synchronization is reapplied when those values change.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A chat connection effect depends on roomId and serverUrl because both determine the external connection. Your goal is to repair stale-closure and runaway-effect defects with correct dependencies. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: State and events; javascript async network; When an effect is needed; javascript scope closures. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Effect dependencies list every reactive value read so synchronization is reapplied when those values change. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Object identity can retrigger effects, suppressing lint hides stale closures, and updater forms can remove a read dependency. This draft therefore rejects: An empty dependency array makes any effect safe to run once with permanently current values.

includes: Declare every reactive dependency and restructure code instead of suppressing the hooks linter.
Repair stale-closure and runaway-effect defects with correct dependencies.
excludes: Object identity can retrigger effects, suppressing lint hides stale closures, and updater forms can remove a read dependency.

outcomes: Repair stale-closure and runaway-effect defects with correct dependencies.

examples.title: Dependencies and reactive values: concrete case
examples.situation: A chat connection effect depends on roomId and serverUrl because both determine the external connection.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A chat connection effect depends on roomId and serverUrl because both determine the external connection.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Dependencies must match reactive reads; an empty array can capture stale initial values.
examples.result: The result follows because Dependencies must match reactive reads; an empty array can capture stale initial values.

examples.title: Dependencies and reactive values: boundary case
examples.situation: Object identity can retrigger effects, suppressing lint hides stale closures, and updater forms can remove a read dependency.

Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: An empty dependency array makes any effect safe to run once with permanently current values.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Dependencies must match reactive reads; an empty array can capture stale initial values.
